title: Regular expressions should avoid containing empty groups
description: Regular expressions should not contain empty groupsAnalyze your codeintentionality-clearmaintainabilityCode Smellregextype-dependentWhy is this an issue?There are several reasons to use a group in a regular expression:to change the precedence (e.g.do(g|or)will match 'dog' and 'door')to remember parenthesised part of the match in the case of capturing groupto improve readabilityIn any case, having an empty group is most probably a mistake. Either it is a leftover after refactoring and should be removed, or the actual parentheses were intended and were not escaped.Noncompliant code exampleconst dateRegex = /^(?:0[1-9]|[12][0-9]|3[01])[- /.](?:0[1-9]|1[012])[- /.](?:19|20)\d\d(?:)$/; // Noncompliant, useless group at the end const methodCallRegex = /foo()/; // Noncompliant, will match only 'f
recommendation: Remove the problematic code and refactor as needed.

checks.message: Regular expressions should avoid containing empty groups - remove () or escape as \(\)
